action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval.py crashed on LPIPS device mismatch (CPU tensors from numpy vs CUDA lpips_fn) during first offset test; prior tensor_to_rgba_pil had channel>4 error (wrong renderer output key/shape like features instead of RGB). Best LPIPS still 0.6021 (partial fallback orbit). Analysis: Custom per-target rays (rel_az + offset) + validated renderer(model.decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) + robust output extraction (try keys, [:3] for >3 chans, CHW/HWC handling) + white-BG composite is correct per inspect; device bug and key selection fragility caused crashes/poor metrics. Sweep over azimuth_offsets will identify best alignment (dataset input pose not at az=0). Zero-shot TripoSR+pose match optimal. Decision: Rewrite complete val_eval.py with fixed compute_metrics (.to('cuda')), robust get_rgb_from_output+tensor_to_rgba_pil (debug prints, all shapes, fallback), offset sweep on 10 val objs (faster), report best offset+metrics. Then port best_offset to predict.py, run full val test, submit_eval for improved LPIPS.
